name: FIRE Enthusiast Frank

assumptions.statement: Finds existing retirement calculators too conservative
assumptions.rationale: Most calculators assume traditional retirement age and 4% rule

assumptions.statement: Willing to pay $50-100 for comprehensive FIRE planning software
assumptions.rationale: Many FIRE enthusiasts are cost-conscious by nature

assumptions.statement: Frustrated by lack of Monte Carlo simulation in free tools
Wants to model side hustle income in retirement
assumptions.rationale: Many FIRE folks plan barista FIRE or coast FIRE

pain_points: Existing tools don't account for aggressive savings rates
No good visualization of FIRE timeline trade-offs
Can't model variable retirement spending (early years vs. later)
Monte Carlo simulations require paid software
Hard to compare multiple FIRE strategies side-by-side
behaviors: Tracks net worth monthly in spreadsheets
Uses Personal Capital or Mint for portfolio tracking
Reads Mr. Money Mustache, Mad Fientist, etc.
Optimizes tax strategies (Roth conversion ladder, etc.)
jobs_to_be_done: When I get a raise, I want to model impact on FIRE timeline, so I can decide how much to increase lifestyle vs. savings
When market crashes, I want to stress-test my plan, so I know if I need to adjust
When planning career moves, I want to compare total comp impact on FIRE date, so I can make informed decisions
When considering geo-arbitrage, I want to model cost-of-living impact, so I can optimize location
